7: def create_map_processed():
8:     processed_dir = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/processed"
9:     clean_dir = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/clean"
10:     output_dir = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps"
11:     os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
12:     
13:     # Shape Data (from data/clean)
14:     lga_shp = os.path.join(clean_dir, "nga_shp/nga_admin2.shp")
15:     
16:     # Processed Data (from data/processed)
17:     conflict_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "conflict.csv")
18:     schools_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "schools.csv")
19:     idp_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "IDP.csv")
20:     summary_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "nga_bay_lga_summary.csv")
21:     
22:     output_map = os.path.join(output_dir, "education_risk_map.html")
23:     
24:     if not all(os.path.exists(f) for f in [lga_shp, summary_csv, conflict_csv, schools_csv, idp_csv]):
25:         print("Missing required spatial or tabular files for mapping.")
26:         return
27: 
28:     print("Loading datasets (Boundaries from clean, points/metrics from processed)...")
29:     # Load boundaries (from data/clean)
30:     gdf_lgas = gpd.read_file(lga_shp)
31:     gdf_lgas["LGA"] = gdf_lgas["adm2_name"].replace({"Tarmua": "Tarmuwa"})
32:     
33:     # Load summary metrics (from data/processed)
34:     df_metrics = pd.read_csv(summary_csv)
35:     
36:     # Merge boundaries with summary metrics
37:     print("Merging boundaries with summary metrics...")
38:     gdf_merged = gdf_lgas.merge(df_metrics, on="LGA", how="inner")
39:     gdf_merged = gdf_merged.to_crs("EPSG:4326")
40:     
41:     # Round risk score for display
42:     gdf_merged["risk_score_disp"] = gdf_merged["risk_score"].round(4)
43:     
44:     # Initialize Folium Map
45:     m = folium.Map(
46:         location=[11.5, 12.5], 
47:         zoom_start=7, 
48:         tiles="cartodbpositron",
49:         control_scale=True
50:     )
51:     
52:     # Define color scale for Risk Score
53:     colormap = cm.LinearColormap(
54:         colors=['#2ca02c', '#ff7f0e', '#d62728'], # Green -> Orange -> Red
55:         vmin=0.0,
56:         vmax=1.0,
57:         caption="Normalized LGA Risk Score (0 = Low, 1 = High)"
58:     )
59:     m.add_child(colormap)
60:     
61:     # Style function for risk score choropleth
62:     def style_fn(feature):
63:         score = feature["properties"]["risk_score"]
64:         return {
65:             "fillColor": colormap(score) if pd.notnull(score) else "#ececec",
66:             "color": "#333333",
67:             "weight": 1.2,
68:             "fillOpacity": 0.55
69:         }
70:         
71:     def highlight_fn(feature):
72:         return {
73:             "weight": 2.5,
74:             "color": "#000000",
75:             "fillOpacity": 0.75
76:         }
77: 
78:     # Add GeoJson Choropleth layer
79:     folium.GeoJson(
80:         gdf_merged,
81:         name="LGA Vulnerability (Risk Score)",
82:         style_function=style_fn,
83:         highlight_function=highlight_fn,
84:         tooltip=folium.GeoJsonTooltip(
85:             fields=["LGA", "state", "risk_score_disp", "school_age_population", "idp_population", "closed_schools"],
86:             aliases=["LGA Name:", "State:", "Risk Score (0-1):", "School-Age Pop:", "IDP Pop:", "Closed Schools:"],
87:             localize=True,
88:             sticky=False,
89:             labels=True,
90:             style="""
91:                 background-color: #F0F2F6;
92:                 border: 2px solid #5F6368;
93:                 font-family: sans-serif;
94:                 font-size: 13px;
95:                 padding: 10px;
96:                 border-radius: 4px;
97:             """
98:         )
99:     ).add_to(m)
100: 
101:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
102:     # Add Points layers from data/processed
103:     # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
104:     
105:     # 1. Add Conflict Point layer (from processed)
106:     print("Adding Conflict points from processed data...")
107:     df_conflict = pd.read_csv(conflict_csv)
108:     df_conflict = df_conflict.dropna(subset=["latitude", "longitude"])
109:     
110:     conflict_group = folium.FeatureGroup(name="Conflict Events", show=False)
111:     for idx, row in df_conflict.iterrows():
112:         folium.CircleMarker(
113:             location=[row["latitude"], row["longitude"]],
114:             radius=1.8,
115:             color="#ff7f0e", # Orange
116:             fill=True,
117:             fill_color="#ff7f0e",
118:             fill_opacity=0.6,
119:             weight=0,
120:             tooltip=f"Conflict Event (Date: {row.get('event_dates', 'Unknown')})"
121:         ).add_to(conflict_group)
122:     conflict_group.add_to(m)
123: 
124:     # 2. Add IDP Point layer (from processed)
125:     print("Adding IDP sites from processed data...")
126:     df_idp = pd.read_csv(idp_csv)
127:     df_idp = df_idp.dropna(subset=["latitude", "longitude"])
128:     
129:     idp_group = folium.FeatureGroup(name="IDP Sites", show=True)
130:     for idx, row in df_idp.iterrows():
131:         folium.CircleMarker(
132:             location=[row["latitude"], row["longitude"]],
133:             radius=3.0,
134:             color="#9467bd", # Purple
135:             fill=True,
136:             fill_color="#9467bd",
137:             fill_opacity=0.8,
138:             weight=0.6,
139:             edge_color="#ffffff",
140:             tooltip=f"IDP Site (Population: {int(row.get('idp_population', 0)):,})"
141:         ).add_to(idp_group)
142:     idp_group.add_to(m)
143: 
144:     # 3. Add Schools Point layer (from processed)
145:     print("Adding School points from processed data...")
146:     df_schools = pd.read_csv(schools_csv)

6:     clean_dir = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/clean"
7:     os.makedirs(clean_dir, exist_ok=True)

10:     conflict_path = os.path.join(raw_dir, "conflict_data_nga.csv")

25:         
26:         # Save to clean/IDP.csv
27:         df_dtm_filtered.to_csv(idp_out, index=False)
28:         print(f"Saved filtered DTM data to {idp_out} (Shape: {df_dtm_filtered.shape})")
29:         print("DTM state counts:")
30:         print(df_dtm_filtered["State"].value_counts())
31:     else:
32:         print(f"Error: DTM file not found at {dtm_path}")
33:         
34:     # 2. Clean Conflict Data
35:     if os.path.exists(conflict_path):
36:         print(f"\nLoading Conflict CSV data...")
37:         df_conflict = pd.read_csv(conflict_path)
38:         print(f"Original Conflict shape: {df_conflict.shape}")
39:         
40:         # Filter for BAY states in adm_1
41:         bay_conflict_states = ["adamawa state", "borno state", "yobe state"]
42:         df_conflict_filtered = df_conflict[
43:             df_conflict["adm_1"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().isin(bay_conflict_states)
44:         ].copy()
45:         
46:         # Save to clean/conflict.csv
47:         df_conflict_filtered.to_csv(conflict_out, index=False)
48:         print(f"Saved filtered Conflict data to {conflict_out} (Shape: {df_conflict_filtered.shape})")
49:         print("Conflict state counts:")
50:         print(df_conflict_filtered["adm_1"].value_counts())
51:     else:
52:         print(f"Error: Conflict file not found at {conflict_path}")
53: 
54: if __name__ == "__main__":
55:     clean_data()
56:

22:                 continue

25:                 step_idx = step.get("step_index", "?")
26:                 source = step.get("source", "")

29:                 content = step.get("content", "")

33:                 if type_ == "USER_INPUT" and source == "USER_EXPLICIT":
34:                     # Extract request content

36:                     user_req = match.group(1).strip() if match else content.strip()
37:                     entry = (
38:                         f"================================================================================\n"
39:                         f"[{created_at}] [STEP {step_idx}] USER INSTRUCTION:\n"
40:                         f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
41:                         f"{user_req}\n"
42:                     )
43:                     log_entries.append(entry)
44:                     
45:                 # Format Model Responses (Thoughts & Tool Calls)
46:                 elif type_ == "PLANNER_RESPONSE" and source == "MODEL":
47:                     entry_parts = [
48:                         f"================================================================================\n"
49:                         f"[{created_at}] [STEP {step_idx}] AGENT ACTION:\n"
50:                         f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
51:                     ]
52:                     # Check for text content / thoughts
53:                     if content:
54:                         entry_parts.append(f"Response:\n{content.strip()}\n")
55:

58:                         entry_parts.append("Tool Calls:\n")
59:                         for tc in tool_calls:
60:                             tc_name = tc.get("name", "")
61:                             tc_args = tc.get("args", {})
62:                             tc_args_str = json.dumps(tc_args, indent=2)
63:                             entry_parts.append(f"  - Call {tc_name} with arguments:\n{tc_args_str}\n")
64:                             
65:                     log_entries.append("".join(entry_parts))
66:                     
67:                 # Format Tool Outputs

69:                     # This represents the output of a tool

Response:
I have modified the Leaflet map generation script [create_risk_map.py](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/scripts/create_risk_map.py) to meet your new design requirements:

1. **Removed the Global Basemap Tiles:**
   - Initialized the Folium map with `tiles=None` to prevent Leaflet from fetching external global cartographic tiles (such as OpenStreetMap or CartoDB).

2. **Restricted View and Panning to BAY States Only:**
   - Programmatically extracted the bounding box extent of the 65 LGAs of Borno, Adamawa, and Yobe (BAY) states.
   - Constrained the map using Leaflet's `max_bounds=True` and passed the computed boundaries (`min_lat`, `max_lat`, `min_lon`, `max_lon`) along with reasonable `min_zoom` and `max_zoom` settings. This prevents the user from panning or zooming outside the target area.
   - Used `fit_bounds()` on load to center the viewport exactly around the BAY states boundary extent.

The map has been regenerated successfully at [outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html). The logging script was run to record all operations in [data/log/log.txt](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/log/log.txt). You can view the updated map in your browser via Port 8000.

[2026-06-27T00:00:51Z] [STEP 608] USER INSTRUCTION:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
What columns in the IDP files are actually needed for this analysis?
================================================================================
[2026-06-27T00:00:51Z] [STEP 609] AGENT ACTION:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Response:
Based on the data cleaning, aggregation, and map visualization pipeline, the following columns are actually needed from the IDP files:

### 1. From the Clean/Raw IDP Dataset (`data/clean/IDP.csv`):
*   **`Population Category`**: Used to filter and identify actual IDP assessment records, excluding returnees and integrated host populations.
*   **`Latitude` & `Longitude`**: Spatial coordinates used to construct point geometries for mapping and spatial joins.
*   **`Individuals`**: The population count at each IDP site, which is aggregated to compute LGA-level density.

### 2. From the Processed IDP Dataset (`data/processed/IDP.csv` and `data/processed/IDP.geojson`):
Once preprocessed, the dataset is trimmed to contain only three columns:
*   **`latitude` & `longitude`** (renamed from `Latitude` and `Longitude`): Used for plotting point markers on the Leaflet map.
*   **`idp_population`** (renamed from `Individuals`): Used for:
    1. Doing a spatial join with LGA boundaries to sum the IDP populations per LGA (`idp_population`).
    2. Displaying the site-level population in the interactive map marker tooltips.
================================================================================

[2026-06-27T00:10:13Z] [STEP 655] AGENT ACTION:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Response:
I will create a comprehensive, well-structured, and professionally formatted `README.md` file in the workspace root, detailing the title, problem, data sources, methodology, key findings, limitations, and project outputs.
Tool Calls:
  - Call write_to_file with arguments:
{
  "CodeContent": "# Education Disruption and Spatial Risk Vulnerability Index\n### \ud83d\udccd Adamawa, Borno, and Yobe (BAY) States, Northeast Nigeria\n\n---\n\n## 1. Problem Statement\nNortheast Nigeria\u2014specifically the states of Borno, Adamawa, and Yobe (BAY)\u2014has faced over a decade of humanitarian crisis due to conflict and instability. This situation has severely disrupted education access. Schools have been closed, populations displaced, and children left vulnerable. \n\nThis project develops a spatial risk vulnerability map and index at the Local Government Area (LGA) level. It highlights where educational disruption is most severe, helping organizations and decision-makers target resources, prioritize school reopenings, and locate support services.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Data Sources\nThe analysis utilizes four key input datasets to evaluate risk across the 65 LGAs in the BAY states. Apart from the administrative boundary shapes, all working datasets are sourced from the processed data directory:\n\n*   **Conflict Data (ACLED):** Point-level records of security events (e.g., battles, explosions, violence against civilians) filtered geographically and historically.\n    *   *Source file:* `data/processed/conflict.csv` / `data/processed/conflict.geojson`\n*   **Displaced Populations (IOM DTM):** Site-level assessment coordinates and internally displaced person (IDP) counts from the International Organization for Migration.\n    *   *Source file:* `data/processed/IDP.csv` / `data/processed/IDP.geojson`\n*   **School Datasets (GRID3 / iMMAP):** Spatial points containing names and operational status (Open, Closed) of educational facilities.\n    *   *Source file:* `data/processed/schools.csv` / `data/processed/schools.geojson`\n*   **School-Age Demographics (SADD):** Tabular data detailing school-age children population (boys and girls) per LGA.\n    *   *Source file:* `data/processed/school_age_population.csv`\n*   **Administrative Boundaries (OCHA):** LGA level polygon shapefiles for Adamawa, Borno, and Yobe states.\n    *   *Source file:* `data/clean/nga_shp/nga_admin2.shp`\n\n---\n\n## 3. Methodology\nThe pipeline processes points, aggregates counts spatially, and calculates a normalized index:\n\n1.  **Spatial Joins:**\n    *   Point datasets (Conflict, IDPs, Schools) are projected to WGS 84 (`EPSG:4326`) and joined with LGA boundary polygons to aggregate event counts, total site populations, and school statuses per LGA.\n2.  **Indicators & Normalization:**\n    *   **School Closure Rate:** `closed_schools / (total_schools + 1)` (to prevent division by zero).\n    *   **Conflict Intensity:** Normalized LGA-level conflict count (0 to 1 scale).\n    *   **IDP Density:** Normalized LGA-level IDP site population (0 to 1 scale).\n    *   **School-Age Density:** Normalized LGA-level school-age children population (0 to 1 scale).\n3.  **Risk Score Formulation:**\n    *   A composite index is calculated using weighted indicators:\n        $$\\text{Risk Score} = (\\text{Conflict Intensity} \\times 0.30) + (\\text{IDP Density} \\times 0.25) + (\\text{School Closure Rate} \\times 0.25) + (\\text{School-Age Density} \\times 0.20)$$\n    *   The final risk score is normalized between 0 (lowest disruption risk) and 1 (highest disruption risk).\n\n---\n\n## 4. Key Insight\n> [!IMPORTANT]\n> **Education disruption is highest where large displaced populations and school closures overlap.**\n> LGAs like **Gwoza**, **Bama**, and **Jere** in Borno State exhibit the highest risk scores because they host both massive IDP camps and have high school closure rates combined with high conflict density. Conversely, high-population urban centers like Maiduguri remain vulnerable due to demographic density, even with fewer school closures.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Limitations & Assumptions\n*   **Data Gaps:** Point coordinates for schools and conflict events are subject to database collection coverage limits and security challenges in reporting. Some remote areas might have underreported events.\n*   **Normalization Assumptions:** Min-Max normalization assumes a linear increase in risk with density. In reality, risk levels might follow non-linear patterns (e.g., threshold effects where risk increases sharply after a certain capacity is reached).\n*   **Coordinate Precision:** IDP coordinates were filtered to remove a site recorded outside the BAY states bounding box (located in Taraba State but mislabeled as Yobe).\n\n---\n\n## 6. Outputs and Deliverables\n\nAll outputs are saved in the project repository:\n\n1.  **Interactive Choropleth Web Map:**\n    *   **Path:** [outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html)\n    *   **Features:** Displays LGA vulnerability using a Green (low) $\\rightarrow$ Orange $\\rightarrow$ Red (high) color scale, overlaying conflict events, IDP sites, and school markers. Zooming and panning is constrained strictly to the BAY states, and LGA boundaries are bordered in black for permanent visibility.\n2.  **Consolidated Summary Dataset:**\n    *   **Path:** [outputs/maps/final_dataset.csv](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/final_dataset.csv)\n    *   *Columns:* `LGA`, `state`, `risk_score`, `school_age_population`, `idp_population`, `conflict_count`, `closed_schools`\n3.  **Top 10 Most Vulnerable LGAs:**\n    *   **Path:** [outputs/maps/top_10_lgas.csv](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/top_10_lgas.csv)\n4.  **Local Landing Page Portal:**\n    *   **Path:** [index.html](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/index.html) (Serves as the main dashboard containing the embedded map and direct links to the CSV downloads).\n",
  "Description": "Create README.md with detailed sections on project problem, data, methodology, limitations, and outputs.",
  "Overwrite": true,
  "TargetFile": "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/README.md",
  "toolAction": "Creating README.md in project root",
  "toolSummary": "Write README.md"
}

I have successfully implemented all requested improvements to the interactive risk map.

### Summary of Improvements Made
1. **New Color Scale:**
   - Switched the choropleth color scale from Green $\rightarrow$ Orange $\rightarrow$ Red to the standard `"YlOrRd"` (Yellow $\rightarrow$ Orange $\rightarrow$ Red) scale. This gives the map a clean, high-contrast heat-map aesthetic for displaying risk levels.
2. **Simplified and Renamed Layer Controls:**
   - Renamed point feature groups so they show up under the Layer Control check-list exactly as requested:
     * `"Schools"`
     * `"IDPs"`
     * `"Conflict"`
   - Each group is individually toggleable on/off, with the permanent LGA wireframe outlines remaining underneath.
3. **Adjusted Marker Sizes:**
   - **Schools**: Changed radius to **`3`** pixels (previously `2.0`).
   - **IDPs**: Changed radius to **`5`** pixels (previously `3.0`).
   - **Conflict**: Changed radius to **`2`** pixels (previously `1.8`).
4. **School Status Popups:**
   - Added interactive click popups to each school marker showing its status (e.g., `School Status: Open` or `School Status: Closed`).
5. **Centered Map Title Banner:**
   - Injected a professional, centered banner at the top of the map displaying:
     `Education Disruption Risk Map – BAY States`
   - Designed the banner with a semi-transparent background, subtle backdrop blur, and modern typography to match the landing page theme.

The improved map is saved at [outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/education_risk_map.html). [2026-06-27T00:13:45Z] [STEP 685] AGENT ACTION:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Response:
Here is the Python code written to perform these calculations, followed by the computed statistical results.

### Python Code
The code is saved in the repository as [scripts/calculate_metrics_summary.py](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/scripts/calculate_metrics_summary.py):

```python
import os
import pandas as pd

def calculate_summary():
    summary_path = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/processed/nga_bay_lga_summary.csv"
    
    if not os.path.exists(summary_path):
        print(f"Error: summary dataset not found at {summary_path}")
        return
        
    df = pd.read_csv(summary_path)
    
    # 1. Total IDP population
    total_idps = int(df["idp_population"].sum())
    
    # 2. Total schools
    total_schools = int(df["total_schools"].sum())
    
    # 3. Closed school ratio = total closed / total schools
    total_closed = int(df["closed_schools"].sum())
    if total_schools > 0:
        closed_school_ratio = total_closed / total_schools
    else:
        closed_school_ratio = 0.0
        
    # 4. Highest risk LGA (max risk_score)
    idx_max = df["risk_score"].idxmax()
    highest_risk_row = df.loc[idx_max]
    highest_risk_lga = highest_risk_row["LGA"]
    highest_risk_score = highest_risk_row["risk_score"]
    highest_risk_state = highest_risk_row["state"]
    
    # Print results clearly
    print("==================================================")
    print("        BAY STATES SUMMARY STATISTICAL METRICS     ")
    print("==================================================")
    print(f"Total IDPs (aggregated):  {total_idps:,}")
    print(f"Total Schools (recorded):  {total_schools:,}")
    print(f"Total Closed Schools:      {total_closed:,}")
    print(f"Closed School Ratio:       {closed_school_ratio:.4f} ({closed_school_ratio * 100:.2f}%)")
    print(f"Highest Risk LGA:          {highest_risk_lga} ({highest_risk_state} State)")
    print(f"  - Risk Score:            {highest_risk_score:.6f}")
    print("==================================================")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    calculate_summary()
```

### Calculated Results

```text
==================================================
        BAY STATES SUMMARY STATISTICAL METRICS     
==================================================
Total IDPs (aggregated):  2,061,563
Total Schools (recorded):  2,152
Total Closed Schools:      299
Closed School Ratio:       0.1389 (13.89%)
Highest Risk LGA:          Gwoza (Borno State)
  - Risk Score:            1.000000
==================================================
```

All metrics and steps are documented in the project log [data/log/log.txt](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/log/log.txt).

  "CodeContent": "import os\nimport pandas as pd\nimport matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n\ndef generate_chart():\n    summary_path = \"/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/processed/nga_bay_lga_summary.csv\"\n    output_path = \"/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/top_lgas_chart.png\"\n    \n    if not os.path.exists(summary_path):\n        print(f\"Error: summary dataset not found at {summary_path}\")\n        return\n        \n    df = pd.read_csv(summary_path)\n    \n    # Sort and select top 10\n    top_10 = df.sort_values(by=\"risk_score\", ascending=False).head(10)\n    \n    # Apply a modern clean dark/dim theme to the plot\n    plt.style.use('dark_background')\n    \n    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6), facecolor=\"#0b0f19\")\n    ax.set_facecolor(\"#0f172a\") # Lighter dark-blue card background\n    \n    # Create colors using a color map corresponding to the risk scores (Yellow -> Orange -> Red)\n    # We scale the index to match the YlOrRd color range nicely\n    colors = plt.cm.YlOrRd(top_10[\"risk_score\"] * 0.75 + 0.25)\n    \n    # Generate the bar plot\n    bars = ax.bar(\n        top_10[\"LGA\"], \n        top_10[\"risk_score\"], \n        color=colors, \n        edgecolor=\"rgba(255,255,255,0.15)\", \n        width=0.55, \n        zorder=3\n    )\n    \n    # Customize grid\n    ax.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.15, zorder=0)\n    \n    # Add title and labels\n    ax.set_title(\"Top 10 High-Risk LGAs\", fontsize=18, fontweight=\"bold\", pad=20, color=\"#f3f4f6\")\n    ax.set_ylabel(\"Risk Score (0 - 1)\", fontsize=12, labelpad=10, color=\"#9ca3af\")\n    ax.set_xlabel(\"Local Government Area (LGA)\", fontsize=12, labelpad=10, color=\"#9ca3af\")\n    \n    # Rotate X labels and set custom styles\n    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=11, color=\"#e5e7eb\")\n    plt.yticks(fontsize=10, color=\"#9ca3af\")\n    \n    # Remove top and right borders (spines)\n    for spine in [\"top\", \"right\"]:\n        ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)\n    ax.spines[\"left\"].set_color(\"rgba(255,255,255,0.1)\")\n    ax.spines[\"bottom\"].set_color(\"rgba(255,255,255,0.1)\")\n    \n    # Add data values on top of each bar\n    for bar in bars:\n        height = bar.get_height()\n        ax.annotate(\n            f\"{height:.3f}\",\n            xy=(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, height),\n            xytext=(0, 5),  # 5 points vertical offset\n            textcoords=\"offset points\",\n            ha='center', \n            va='bottom', \n            fontsize=10, \n            fontweight=\"bold\", \n            color=\"#f3f4f6\"\n        )\n                    \n    # Save the figure\n    plt.tight_layout()\n    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)\n    plt.savefig(output_path, dpi=300, facecolor=fig.get_facecolor(), bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n    plt.close()\n    print(f\"Chart generated and saved successfully to {output_path}\")\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    generate_chart()\n",

Response:
Here is the Python code written to generate the bar chart and information about the saved chart image:

### Python Code
The code is saved in the repository as [scripts/create_risk_chart.py](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/scripts/create_risk_chart.py):

```python
import os
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def generate_chart():
    summary_path = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/processed/nga_bay_lga_summary.csv"
    output_path = "/workspaces/NigeriaBAY/outputs/maps/top_lgas_chart.png"
    
    if not os.path.exists(summary_path):
        print(f"Error: summary dataset not found at {summary_path}")
        return
        
    df = pd.read_csv(summary_path)
    
    # Sort and select top 10
    top_10 = df.sort_values(by="risk_score", ascending=False).head(10)
    
    # Apply a modern clean dark/dim theme to the plot
    plt.style.use('dark_background')
    
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6), facecolor="#0b0f19")
    ax.set_facecolor("#0f172a") # Lighter dark-blue card background
    
    # Create colors using a color map corresponding to the risk scores (Yellow -> Orange -> Red)
    colors = plt.cm.YlOrRd(top_10["risk_score"] * 0.75 + 0.25)
    
    # Generate the bar plot
    bars = ax.bar(
        top_10["LGA"], 
        top_10["risk_score"], 
        color=colors, 
        edgecolor=(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.15), 
        width=0.55, 
        zorder=3
    )
    
    # Customize grid
    ax.grid(axis='y', linestyle='--', alpha=0.15, zorder=0)
    
    # Add title and labels
    ax.set_title("Top 10 High-Risk LGAs", fontsize=18, fontweight="bold", pad=20, color="#f3f4f6")
    ax.set_ylabel("Risk Score (0 - 1)", fontsize=12, labelpad=10, color="#9ca3af")
    ax.set_xlabel("Local Government Area (LGA)", fontsize=12, labelpad=10, color="#9ca3af")
    
    # Rotate X labels and set custom styles
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=11, color="#e5e7eb")
    plt.yticks(fontsize=10, color="#9ca3af")
    
    # Remove top and right borders (spines)
    for spine in ["top", "right"]:
        ax.spines[spine].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["left"].set_color((1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.1))
    ax.spines["bottom"].set_color((1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 0.1))
    
    # Add data values on top of each bar
    for bar in bars:
        height = bar.get_height()
        ax.annotate(
            f"{height:.3f}",
            xy=(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, height),
            xytext=(0, 5),  # 5 points vertical offset
            textcoords="offset points",
            ha='center', 
            va='bottom', 
            fontsize=10, 
            fontweight="bold", 
            color="#f3f4f6"
        )
                    
    # Save the figure
    plt.tight_layout()
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)
    plt.savefig(output_path, dpi=300, facecolor=fig.get_facecolor(), bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()
    print(f"Chart generated and saved successfully to {output_path}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    generate_chart()
```

          Core Insight\n            </div>\n            <p class=\"insight-text\">\n                \"Education disruption is concentrated in LGAs where conflict activity, high IDP populations, and school closures overlap. These LGAs should be prioritized for intervention.\"\n            </p>\n        </section>\n\n        <!-- Downloads bar -->\n        <section class=\"downloads-bar\">\n            <a href=\"outputs/maps/final_dataset.csv\" class=\"btn-download\" target=\"_blank\">\n                <svg width=\"16\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4\"></path>\n                    <polyline points=\"7 10 12 15 17 10\"></polyline>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"15\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"3\"></line>\n                </svg>\n                Full Dataset (.csv)\n            </a>\n            <a href=\"outputs/maps/top_10_lgas.csv\" class=\"btn-download\" target=\"_blank\">\n                <svg width=\"16\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4\"></path>\n                    <polyline points=\"7 10 12 15 17 10\"></polyline>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"15\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"3\"></line>\n                </svg>\n                Top 10 LGAs List (.csv)\n            </a>\n        </section>\n    </div>\n\n    <!-- Data Injection -->\n    <script>\n        const rawLgaData = {lga_data_json};\n        \n        let chartInstance = null;\n\n        function updateDashboard(state) {{\n            // 1. Filter Data\n            const filteredData = state === \"All\" \n                ? rawLgaData \n                : rawLgaData.filter(item => item.state === state);\n\n            // 2. Compute KPIs\n            const totalIdps = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.idp_population || 0), 0);\n            const totalSchools = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.total_schools || 0), 0);\n            const totalClosed = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.closed_schools || 0), 0);\n            \n            const closedRatio = totalSchools > 0 \n                ? ((totalClosed / totalSchools) * 100).toFixed(2) + \"%\"\n                : \"0.00%\";\n\n            // Find highest risk LGA in this subset\n            let highestRiskLga = \"N/A\";\n            let highestRiskScore = -1;\n            filteredData.forEach(item => {{\n                if (item.risk_score > highestRiskScore) {{\n                    highestRiskScore = item.risk_score;\n                    highestRiskLga = item.LGA;\n                }}\n            }});\n\n            // 3. Update DOM KPI Values\n            document.getElementById(\"val-idps\").textContent = totalIdps.toLocaleString();\n            document.getElementById(\"val-schools\").textContent = totalSchools.toLocaleString();\n            document.getElementById(\"val-closed-ratio\").textContent = closedRatio;\n            \n            const highRiskValNode = document.getElementById(\"val-high-risk\");\n            highRiskValNode.textContent = highestRiskLga;\n            // Shrink text slightly if name is too long to prevent spillover\n            if (highestRiskLga.length > 12) {{\n                highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = \"1.8rem\";\n            }} else {{\n                highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = \"2.2rem\";\n            }}\n\n            const labelHighRisk = document.getElementById(\"label-high-risk\");\n            labelHighRisk.textContent = state === \"All\" ? \"Highest Risk LGA\" : `Highest Risk LGA in ${{state}}`;\n\n            // 4. Update Chart (Top 10 LGAs by Risk Score)\n            // Sort by risk_score desc and take top 10\n            const top10 = [...filteredData]\n                .sort((a, b) => b.risk_score - a.risk_score)\n                .slice(0, 10);\n\n            const labels = top10.map(item => item.LGA);\n            const scores = top10.map(item => item.risk_score);\n\n            updateChart(labels, scores);\n        }}\n\n        function updateChart(labels, scores) {{\n            const ctx = document.getElementById('risk-chart').getContext('2d');\n            \n            // Build dynamic colors corresponding to YlOrRd colormap\n            const colors = scores.map(score => {{\n                // Interpolate colors between yellow (low score) and red (high score)\n                // Yellow: rgba(255, 237, 160, 0.85) -> Red: rgba(189, 0, 38, 0.9)\n                const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);\n                const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);\n                const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\n                return `rgba(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}}, 0.85)`;\n            }});\n\n            const borderColors = scores.map(score => {{\n                const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);\n                const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);\n                const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\n                return `rgb(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}})`;\n            }});\n\n            if (chartInstance) {{\n                chartInstance.data.labels = labels;\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].data = scores;\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].backgroundColor = colors;\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].borderColor = borderColors;\n                chartInstance.update();\n            }} else {{\n                chartInstance = new Chart(ctx, {{\n                    type: 'bar',\n                    data: {{\n [2026-06-27T00:18:55Z] [STEP 718] AGENT ACTION:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Response:
I have updated the dashboard to make it fully dynamic and resolve the visibility issue with the top 10 chart.

### What Was Updated
1. **Dynamic Interactive Charting (Chart.js):**
   - Swapped the static matplotlib PNG image with an interactive canvas chart using **Chart.js**.
   - This fixes the sizing/readability issue: the chart is now fully responsive, features custom hover tooltips showing precise risk values, and labels adjust dynamically for readability.
   - The bar colors interpolate dynamically along the `"YlOrRd"` color ramp (yellow for lower risk, red for higher risk).
2. **State Selection Dropdown:**
   - Added a dropdown selector at the top of the dashboard:
     * **All States (BAY Region)**
     * **Adamawa**
     * **Borno**
     * **Yobe**
3. **Dynamic Javascript Engine:**
   - Injected the consolidated LGA metrics data as a JSON object directly inside [index.html](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/index.html).
   - When you toggle the dropdown, the dashboard automatically filters the dataset in real-time, recalculates and updates the values in the KPI cards (Total IDPs, Total Schools, % Closed Schools, and Highest Risk LGA), and replots the Top 10 High-Risk LGAs specifically for that selected state.

You can experience the updated dashboard immediately by refreshing http://localhost:8000/index.html in your browser. All pipeline actions are logged in [data/log/log.txt](file:///workspaces/NigeriaBAY/data/log/log.txt). Let me know if there's anything else you'd like to adjust!

12:     
13:     # Shape Data (from data/clean)
14:     lga_shp = os.path.join(clean_dir, "nga_shp/nga_admin2.shp")
15:     
16:     # Processed Data (from data/processed)
17:     conflict_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "conflict.csv")

19:     idp_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "IDP.csv")
20:     summary_csv = os.path.join(processed_dir, "nga_bay_lga_summary.csv")
21:     
22:     output_map = os.path.join(output_dir, "education_risk_map.html")
23:     
24:     if not all(os.path.exists(f) for f in [lga_shp, summary_csv, conflict_csv, schools_csv, idp_csv]):
25:         print("Missing required spatial or tabular files for mapping.")
26:         return
27: 
28:     print("Loading datasets (Boundaries from clean, points/metrics from processed)...")
29:     # Load boundaries (from data/clean)
30:     gdf_lgas = gpd.read_file(lga_shp)
31:     gdf_lgas["LGA"] = gdf_lgas["adm2_name"].replace({"Tarmua": "Tarmuwa"})
32:     
33:     # Load summary metrics (from data/processed)
34:     df_metrics = pd.read_csv(summary_csv)
35:     
36:     # Merge boundaries with summary metrics
37:     print("Merging boundaries with summary metrics...")
38:     gdf_merged = gdf_lgas.merge(df_metrics, on="LGA", how="inner")
39:     gdf_merged = gdf_merged.to_crs("EPSG:4326")
40:     
41:     # Calculate bounds for map restrictions (removing the rest of the country/world)
42:     bounds = gdf_merged.total_bounds  # [lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max]
43:     pad = 0.4
44:     min_lon = float(bounds[0] - pad)
45:     min_lat = float(bounds[1] - pad)
46:     max_lon = float(bounds[2] + pad)
47:     max_lat = float(bounds[3] + pad)
48:     
49:     center_lat = (min_lat + max_lat) / 2
50:     center_lon = (min_lon + max_lon) / 2
51:     
52:     # Round risk score for display
53:     gdf_merged["risk_score_disp"] = gdf_merged["risk_score"].round(4)
54:     
55:     # Initialize Folium Map restricted only to BAY states
56:     m = folium.Map(
57:         location=[center_lat, center_lon], 
58:         zoom_start=7, 
59:         tiles=None,
60:         control_scale=True,
61:         min_lat=min_lat,
62:         max_lat=max_lat,
63:         min_lon=min_lon,
64:         max_lon=max_lon,
65:         max_bounds=True,
66:         min_zoom=6,
67:         max_zoom=12
68:     )
69:     
70:     # Fit map bounds to the BAY states extent
71:     m.fit_bounds([[min_lat, min_lon], [max_lat, max_lon]])
72:     
73:     # Define color scale for Risk Score (Yellow -> Orange -> Red)
74:     colormap = cm.linear.YlOrRd_09.scale(0.0, 1.0)
75:     colormap.caption = "Normalized LGA Risk Score (0 = Low, 1 = High)"
76:     m.add_child(colormap)
77: 
78:     # Add title banner to the map
79:     title_html = """
80:     <div style="position: fixed; 
81:                 top: 15px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%);
82:                 z-index: 9999; font-size: 18px;
83:                 background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);

91:                 text-align: center;">
92:         Education Disruption Risk Map &ndash; BAY States
93:     </div>
94:     """
95:     m.get_root().html.add_child(folium.Element(title_html))
96:     
97:     # Style function for risk score choropleth
98:     def style_fn(feature):
99:         score = feature["properties"]["risk_score"]
100:         return {
101:             "fillColor": colormap(score) if pd.notnull(score) else "#ececec",
102:             "color": "#000000", # Black outline
103:             "weight": 1.2,
104:             "fillOpacity": 0.55
105:         }
106:         
107:     def highlight_fn(feature):
108:         return {
109:             "weight": 2.5,
110:             "color": "#000000",
111:             "fillOpacity": 0.75
112:         }
113: 
114:     # Add permanent static LGA boundaries wireframe (always visible)
115:     folium.GeoJson(
116:         gdf_merged,
117:         style_function=lambda x: {
118:             "fillColor": "transparent",
119:             "color": "#000000", # Black outline
120:             "weight": 1.2,
121:             "fillOpacity": 0.0
122:         },
123:         control=False,
124:         interactive=False
125:     ).add_to(m)
126:

128:     folium.GeoJson(

171:     conflict_group.add_to(m)
172: 
173:     # 2. Add IDP Point layer (from processed)
174:     print("Adding IDP sites from processed data...")
175:     df_idp = pd.read_csv(idp_csv)
176:     df_idp = df_idp.dropna(subset=["latitude", "longitude"])
177:     
178:     idp_group = folium.FeatureGroup(name="IDPs", show=True)

180:         folium.CircleMarker(

190:         ).add_to(idp_group)

502:     <!-- Data Injection -->
503:     <script>
504:         const rawLgaData = {lga_data_json};
505:         
506:         let chartInstance = null;
507: 
508:         function updateDashboard(state) {{
509:             // 1. Filter Data
510:             const filteredData = state === "All" 
511:                 ? rawLgaData 
512:                 : rawLgaData.filter(item => item.state === state);
513: 
514:             // 2. Compute KPIs
515:             const totalIdps = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.idp_population || 0), 0);
516:             const totalSchools = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.total_schools || 0), 0);
517:             const totalClosed = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) => sum + (item.closed_schools || 0), 0);
518:             
519:             const closedRatio = totalSchools > 0 
520:                 ? ((totalClosed / totalSchools) * 100).toFixed(2) + "%"
521:                 : "0.00%";
522: 
523:             // Find highest risk LGA in this subset
524:             let highestRiskLga = "N/A";
525:             let highestRiskScore = -1;
526:             filteredData.forEach(item => {{
527:                 if (item.risk_score > highestRiskScore) {{
528:                     highestRiskScore = item.risk_score;
529:                     highestRiskLga = item.LGA;
530:                 }}
531:             }});
532: 
533:             // 3. Update DOM KPI Values
534:             document.getElementById("val-idps").textContent = totalIdps.toLocaleString();
535:             document.getElementById("val-schools").textContent = totalSchools.toLocaleString();
536:             document.getElementById("val-closed-ratio").textContent = closedRatio;
537:             
538:             const highRiskValNode = document.getElementById("val-high-risk");
539:             highRiskValNode.textContent = highestRiskLga;
540:             // Shrink text slightly if name is too long to prevent spillover
541:             if (highestRiskLga.length > 12) {{
542:                 highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = "1.8rem";
543:             }} else {{
544:                 highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = "2.2rem";
545:             }}
546: 
547:             const labelHighRisk = document.getElementById("label-high-risk");
548:             labelHighRisk.textContent = state === "All" ? "Highest Risk LGA" : `Highest Risk LGA in ${{state}}`;
549: 
550:             // 4. Update Chart (Top 10 LGAs by Risk Score)
551:             // Sort by risk_score desc and take top 10
552:             const top10 = [...filteredData]
553:                 .sort((a, b) => b.risk_score - a.risk_score)
554:                 .slice(0, 10);
555: 
556:             const labels = top10.map(item => item.LGA);
557:             const scores = top10.map(item => item.risk_score);
558: 
559:             updateChart(labels, scores);
560:         }}
561: 
562:         function updateChart(labels, scores) {{
563:             const ctx = document.getElementById('risk-chart').getContext('2d');
564:             
565:             // Build dynamic colors corresponding to YlOrRd colormap
566:             const colors = scores.map(score => {{
567:                 // Interpolate colors between yellow (low score) and red (high score)
568:                 // Yellow: rgba(255, 237, 160, 0.85) -> Red: rgba(189, 0, 38, 0.9)
569:                 const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);
570:                 const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);
571:                 const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);
572:                 return `rgba(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}}, 0.85)`;
573:             }});
574: 
575:             const borderColors = scores.map(score => {{
576:                 const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);
577:                 const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);
578:                 const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);
579:                 return `rgb(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}})`;
580:             }});
581: 
582:             if (chartInstance) {{
583:                 chartInstance.data.labels = labels;
584:                 chartInstance.data.datasets[0].data = scores;
585:                 chartInstance.data.datasets[0].backgroundColor = colors;
586:                 chartInstance.data.datasets[0].borderColor = borderColors;
587:                 chartInstance.update();
588:             }} else {{
589:                 chartInstance = new Chart(ctx, {{
590:                     type: 'bar',
591:                     data: {{
592:                         labels: labels,
593:                         datasets: [{{
594:                             label: 'Risk Score',
595:                             data: scores,
596:                             backgroundColor: colors,
597:                             borderColor: borderColors,
598:                             borderWidth: 1.5,
599:                             borderRadius: 6,
600:                             barPercentage: 0.6
601:                         }}]
602:                     }},
603:                     options: {{
604:                         responsive: true,
605:                         maintainAspectRatio: false,
606:                         plugins: {{
607:                             legend: {{
608:                                 display: false
609:                             }},
610:                             tooltip: {{
611:                                 backgroundColor: '#1e293b',
612:                                 titleColor: '#f3f4f6',
613:                                 bodyColor: '#9ca3af',
614:                                 borderColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',
615:                                 borderWidth: 1,
616:                                 padding: 10,
617:                                 font: {{
618:                                     family: "'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif"
619:                                 }},
620:                                 callbacks: {{
621:                                     label: function(context) {{
622:                                         return ` Risk Score: ${{context.raw.toFixed(4)}}`;
623:                                     }}
624:                                 }}
625:                             }}
626:                         }},
627:                         scales: {{
628:                             x: {{
629:                                 grid: {{
630:                                     display: false
631:                                 }},
632:                                 ticks: {{
633:                                     color: '#e5e7eb',
634:                                     font: {{
635:                                         family: "'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif",
636:                                         size: 11,
637:                                         weight: '500'
638:                                     }},
639:                                     maxRotation: 45,
640:                                     minRotation: 45
641:                                 }}
642:                             }},
643:                             y: {{
644:                                 min: 0,
645:                                 max: 1.0,
646:                                 grid: {{
647:                                     color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.06)'
648:                                 }},
649:                                 ticks: {{
650:                                     color: '#9ca3af',
651:                                     font: {{
652:                                         family: "'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif",
653:                                         size: 10
654:                                     }}
655:                                 }}
656:                             }}
657:                         }}
658:                     }}
659:                 }});
660:             }}
661:         }}
662: 
663:         function onStateChange(value) {{
664:             updateDashboard(value);
665:         }}
666: 
667:         // Initialize on load
668:         window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {{
669:             updateDashboard("All");
670:         }});
671:     </script>
672: </body>
673: </html>
674: """
675:     
676:     with open(output_path, "w") as f:
677:         f.write(html_content)
678:         
679:     print(f"Dashboard successfully compiled and written to {output_path}")
680: 
681: if __name__ == "__main__":
682:     build_dashboard()
683: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

          \u003cline x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"17\\\" x2=\\\"12.01\\\" y2=\\\"17\\\"\u003e\u003c/line\u003e\\n                \u003c/svg\u003e\\n                Core Insight\\n            \u003c/div\u003e\\n            \u003cp class=\\\"insight-text\\\"\u003e\\n                \\\"Education disruption is concentrated in LGAs where conflict activity, high IDP populations, and school height=\\\"16\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"\u003e\\n                    \u003cpath d=\\\"M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4\\\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\\n                    \u003cpolyline points=\\\"7 10 12 15 17 10\\\"\u003e\u003c/polyline\u003e\\n                    \u003cline x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"15\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"3\\\"\u003e\u003c/line\u003e\\n                \u003c/svg\u003e\\n                Full Dataset (.csv)\\n            \u003c/a\u003e\\n            \u003ca href=\\\"outputs/maps/top_10_lgas.csv\\\" class=\\\"btn-download\\\" target=\\\"_blank\\\"\u003e\\n                \u003csvg width=\\\"16\\\" height=\\\"16\\\" viewBox=\\\"0 0 24 24\\\" fill=\\\"none\\\" stroke=\\\"currentColor\\\" stroke-width=\\\"2\\\" stroke-linecap=\\\"round\\\" stroke-linejoin=\\\"round\\\"\u003e\\n                    \u003cpath d=\\\"M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4\\\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\\n                    \u003cpolyline points=\\\"7 10 12 15 17 10\\\"\u003e\u003c/polyline\u003e\\n                    \u003cline x1=\\\"12\\\" y1=\\\"15\\\" x2=\\\"12\\\" y2=\\\"3\\\"\u003e\u003c/line\u003e\\n                \u003c/svg\u003e\\n                Top 10 LGAs List (.csv)\\n            \u003c/a\u003e\\n        \u003c/section\u003e\\n    \u003c/div\u003e\\n\\n    \u003c!-- Data Injection --\u003e\\n    \u003cscript\u003e\\n        const rawLgaData = {lga_data_json};\\n        \\n        let chartInstance = null;\\n\\n        function updateDashboard(state) {{\\n            // 1. Filter Data\\n            const filteredData = state === \\\"All\\\" \\n                ? rawLgaData \\n                : rawLgaData.filter(item =\u003e item.state === state);\\n\\n            // 2. Compute KPIs\\n            const totalIdps = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.idp_population || 0), 0);\\n            const totalSchools = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.total_schools || 0), 0);\\n            const totalClosed = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.closed_schools || 0), 0);\\n            \\n            const closedRatio = totalSchools \u003e 0 \\n                ? ((totalClosed / totalSchools) * 100).toFixed(2) + \\\"%\\\"\\n                : \\\"0.00%\\\";\\n\\n            // Find highest risk LGA in this subset\\n            let highestRiskLga = \\\"N/A\\\";\\n            let highestRiskScore = -1;\\n            filteredData.forEach(item =\u003e {{\\n                if (item.risk_score \u003e highestRiskScore) {{\\n                    highestRiskScore = item.risk_score;\\n                    highestRiskLga = item.LGA;\\n                }}\\n            }});\\n\\n            // 3. Update DOM KPI Values\\n            document.getElementById(\\\"val-idps\\\").textContent = totalIdps.toLocaleString();\\n            document.getElementById(\\\"val-schools\\\").textContent = totalSchools.toLocaleString();\\n            document.getElementById(\\\"val-closed-ratio\\\").textContent = closedRatio;\\n            \\n            const highRiskValNode = document.getElementById(\\\"val-high-risk\\\");\\n            highRiskValNode.textContent = highestRiskLga;\\n            // Shrink text slightly if name is too long to prevent spillover\\n            if (highestRiskLga.length \u003e 12) {{\\n                highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = \\\"1.8rem\\\";\\n            }} else {{\\n                highRiskValNode.style.fontSize = \\\"2.2rem\\\";\\n            }}\\n\\n            const labelHighRisk = document.getElementById(\\\"label-high-risk\\\");\\n            labelHighRisk.textContent = state === \\\"All\\\" ? \\\"Highest Risk LGA\\\" : `Highest Risk LGA in ${{state}}`;\\n\\n            // 4. Update Chart (Top 10 LGAs by Risk Score)\\n            // Sort by risk_score desc and take top 10\\n            const top10 = [...filteredData]\\n                .sort((a, b) =\u003e b.risk_score - a.risk_score)\\n                .slice(0, 10);\\n\\n            const labels = top10.map(item =\u003e item.LGA);\\n            const scores = top10.map(item =\u003e item.risk_score);\\n\\n            updateChart(labels, scores);\\n        }}\\n\\n        function updateChart(labels, scores) {{\\n            const ctx = document.getElementById('risk-chart').getContext('2d');\\n            \\n            // Build dynamic colors corresponding to YlOrRd colormap\\n            const colors = scores.map(score =\u003e {{\\n                // Interpolate colors between yellow (low score) and red (high score)\\n                // Yellow: rgba(255, 237, 160, 0.85) -\u003e Red: rgba(189, 0, 38, 0.9)\\n                const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);\\n                const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);\\n                const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\\n                return `rgba(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}}, 0.85)`;\\n            }});\\n\\n            const borderColors = scores.map(score =\u003e {{\\n                const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);\\n                const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);\\n                const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\\n                return `rgb(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}})`;\\n            }});\\n\\n            if (chartInstance) {{\\n                chartInstance.data.labels = labels;\\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].data = scores;\\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].backgroundColor = colors;\\n                chartInstance.data.datasets[0].borderColor = borderColors;\\n                chartInstance.update();\\n            }} else {{\\n                chartInstance = new Chart(ctx, {{\\n                    type: 'bar',\\n                    data: {{\\n                        labels: labels,\\n                        datasets: [{{\\n                            label: 'Risk Score',\\n                            data: scores,\\n                            backgroundColor: colors,\\n                            borderColor: borderColors,\\n                            borderWidth: 1.5,\\n                            borderRadius: 6,\\n                            barPercentage: 0.6\\n                        }}]\\n                    }},\\n                    options: {{\\n                        responsive: true,\\n                        maintainAspectRatio: false,\\n                        plugins: {{\\n                            legend: {{\\n                                display: false\\n                            }},\\n                            tooltip: {{\\n                                backgroundColor: '#1e293b',\\n                                titleColor: '#f3f4f6',\\n                                bodyColor: '#9ca3af',\\n                                borderColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.1)',\\n                                borderWidth: 1,\\n                                padding: 10,\\n                                font: {{\\n                                    family: \\\"'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif\\\"\\n                                }},\\n                                callbacks: {{\\n                                    label: function(context) {{\\n                                        return ` Risk Score: ${{context.raw.toFixed(4)}}`;\\n                                    }}\\n                                }}\\n                            }}\\n                        }},\\n                        scales: {{\\n                            x: {{\\n                                grid: {{\\n                                    display: false\\n                                }},\\n                                ticks: {{\\n                                    color: '#e5e7eb',\\n                                    font: {{\\n                                        family: \\\"'Plus Jakarta updateDashboard(state) {{\\n            // 1. Filter Data\\n            const filteredData = state === \\\"All\\\" \\n                ? rawLgaData \\n                : rawLgaData.filter(item =\u003e item.state === state);\\n\\n            // 2. Compute KPIs\\n            const totalIdps = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.idp_population || 0), 0);\\n            const totalSchools = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.total_schools || 0), 0);\\n            const totalClosed = filteredData.reduce((sum, item) =\u003e sum + (item.closed_schools || 0), 0);\\n            \\n            const closedRatio = totalSchools \u003e 0 \\n                ? ((totalClosed / totalSchools) * 100).toFixed(2) + \\\"%\\\"\\n                : \\\"0.00%\\\";\\n\\n            // Find highest risk LGA in this subset\\n            let highestRiskLga = \\\"N/A\\\";\\n            let highestRiskScore = -1;\\n            filteredData.forEach(item =\u003e {{\\n                if (item.risk_score \u003e highestRiskScore) {{\\n                    highestRiskScore = item.risk_score;\\n                    highestRiskLga = item.LGA;\\n                }}\\n            }});\\n\\n            // 3. Update Chart (Top 10 LGAs by Risk Score)\\n            // Sort by risk_score desc and take top 10\\n            const top10 = [...filteredData]\\n                .sort((a, b) =\u003e b.risk_score - a.risk_score)\\n                .slice(0, 10);\\n\\n            const labels = top10.map(item =\u003e item.LGA);\\n            const scores = top10.map(item =\u003e item.risk_score);\\n\\n      // Build dynamic colors corresponding to YlOrRd colormap\\n            const colors = scores.map(score =\u003e {{\\n                // Interpolate colors between yellow (low score) and red (high score)\\n                // Yellow: rgba(255, 237, 160, 0.85) -\u003e Red: rgba(189, 0, 38, 0.9)\\n                const r = Math.round(255 - (255 - 189) * score);\\n                const g = Math.round(237 - (237 - 0) * score);\\n                const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\\n                return `rgba(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}}, 0.85)`;\\n            }});\\n\\n const b = Math.round(160 - (160 - 38) * score);\\n                return `rgb(${{r}}, ${{g}}, ${{b}})`;\\n            }});\\n\\n            if (chartInstance) {{\\n                       padding: 10,\\n                                font: {{\\n                                    family: \\\"'Plus Jakarta Sans', sans-serif\\\"\\n 11:

13:         print(f"Transcript file not found at {transcript_path}")
14:         return
15: 
16:     print(f"Parsing transcript from {transcript_path}...")
17:     log_entries = []
18:     
19:     with open(transcript_path, 'r') as f:
20:         for line in f:
21:             if not line.strip():
22:                 continue
23:             try:
24:                 step = json.loads(line)
25:                 step_idx = step.get("step_index", "?")
26:                 source = step.get("source", "")
27:                 type_ = step.get("type", "")
28:                 created_at = step.get("created_at", "")
29:                 content = step.get("content", "")
30:                 tool_calls = step.get("tool_calls", [])
31:                 
32:                 # Format User Inputs
33:                 if type_ == "USER_INPUT" and source == "USER_EXPLICIT":
34:                     # Extract request content
35:                     match = re.search(r'<USER_REQUEST>(.*?)</USER_REQUEST>', content, re.DOTALL)
36:                     user_req = match.group(1).strip() if match else content.strip()
37:                     entry = (
38:                         f"================================================================================\n"
39:                         f"[{created_at}] [STEP {step_idx}] USER INSTRUCTION:\n"
40:                         f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
41:                         f"{user_req}\n"
42:                     )
43:                     log_entries.append(entry)
44:                     
45:                 # Format Model Responses (Thoughts & Tool Calls)
46:                 elif type_ == "PLANNER_RESPONSE" and source == "MODEL":
47:                     entry_parts = [
48:                         f"================================================================================\n"
49:                         f"[{created_at}] [STEP {step_idx}] AGENT ACTION:\n"
50:                         f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
51:                     ]
52:                     # Check for text content / thoughts
53:                     if content:
54:                         entry_parts.append(f"Response:\n{content.strip()}\n")
55:                     
56:                     # Check for tool calls
57:                     if tool_calls:
58:                         entry_parts.append("Tool Calls:\n")
59:                         for tc in tool_calls:
60:                             tc_name = tc.get("name", "")
61:                             tc_args = tc.get("args", {})
62:                             tc_args_str = json.dumps(tc_args, indent=2)
63:                             entry_parts.append(f"  - Call {tc_name} with arguments:\n{tc_args_str}\n")
64:                             
65:                     log_entries.append("".join(entry_parts))
66:                     
67:                 # Format Tool Outputs
68:                 elif source == "MODEL" and type_ != "PLANNER_RESPONSE":
69:                     # This represents the output of a tool
70:                     entry = (
71:                         f"================================================================================\n"
72:                         f"[{created_at}] [STEP {step_idx}] TOOL OUTPUT ({type_}):\n"
73:                         f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
74:                         f"{content.strip()}\n"
75:                     )
76:                     log_entries.append(entry)
77:                     
78:                 # Format System checkpoint notifications
79:                 elif source == "SYSTEM" and content:
80:                     # Ignore standard checkpoints to avoid clutter, unless they contain message content
81:                     if "Resuming from a compaction" not in content:
82:                         entry = (
83:                             f"================================================================================\n"
84:                             f"[{created_at}] SYSTEM MESSAGE:\n"
85:                             f"--------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n"
86:                             f"{content.strip()}\n"
87:                         )

89:
